r: će, ćīm, Urdū, ća, Hindī, Hindūstānī, Nāgarī, ch, cherry, jīm-ě-fārsī, jīm-ě-ʻajamī, jīm, abjad, ća-kār
def: s.m. The letter or the sound ća.
more: Persian, called also jīm-i-fārsī or jīm-i-ajmī , the seventh letter of the Persian alphabet, sounds like ch in the English word cheek. It does not occur in Arabic. In abjad q. v. it stands for the number (3), the same as jīm . च cha H. is the sixth consonant in the Hindi alphabet, and the first letter of the chavarg or palatal class. As initial, medial, and final it is interchangable with k , as chasak=kasak ache; chillānā=killānā to squeal; chāchā=kākā uncle; chūṅ=kyoṅ why; chhāl=khāl skin; lachchhan=lakkhan manner; achchhar=akkhar letter; māchhī=mākhī a fly: with sh , as chhaṭ = S. shashṭ , P. shash six; H. chhāyā = Eng. shadow; bichhaṛnā = Brij. bishaṭnā to be separated; P. la/?/khchah = P. la/?/khshah live coal: with p, t, ṭ and j , q. v. As an initial with ḍ , as chapnī=ḍhaknī a cover: with s , as chhobā=sobhā splendour; chhakṛā = Panj. saëḍho cart; chatrāo = satrāo shower: chhajnā …

called also jīm-i-fārsī or jīm-i-aﻌjamī, the seventh letter of the Persian alphabet, sounds like ch in the English word cheek. It does not occur in Arabic. If used to denote a number, according to the abjad, it stands for 3, like ج ( jīm ). The permutations of the letter چ are the following; into ژ as کاژ for کاچ Glass, a pine tree; into ز as پزشک pizishk for پچشک pichishk, A physician or apothecary; into ش as کاشي for کاچي Made of glass; into ج as کاج for کاچ; and, arabistically, into ص as صين for چين China. This letter corresponds to च in Sanskrit, and when aspirated, or with ه affixed, to छ .

def: s.m. = A servant, slave, &c. ( = ćelā, q.v.). the first letter of the Arabic, Persian, and Urdū alphabets. Its pronunciation depends on the vowel which accompanies it; e.g. ا with fatḥa (اَ) = the Devanāgarī अ = the English a, as in cedar (or = the English u in fun); ا with kasra (اِ) = the Devanāgarī इ = the English i, as in fin; ا with ẓamma (اُ) = the Devanāgarī उ = the English u, as in bull. — ا with fatḥa, followed by ا, (i.e. اَا, which is usually written آ) = the Devanāgarī आ = the English ā, as in father; ا with fatḥa, followed by the letter و, (i.e. اَو) = the Devanāgarī औ = the English au (sounded like ou in house); ا with fatḥa, followed by the letter ي, (i.e. اَي), = the Devanāgarī ऐ = the English ai, as in aisle. — ا with ẓamma, followed by the letter و, (i.e. اُو), = 1˚, the Devanāgarī ऊ = the English u, as in rule (or = the oo in pool), and, 2˚, = the Devanāgarī ओ = the English o, as in pole. — ا with kasra, followed by ي, (i.e. اِي), = 1˚, the Devanāgarī ई = the English ī, as in caprice; and, 2˚, = the Devanāgarī ए = the French e, as in fête. ا is used, — 1˚ (arithmetically, according to the Abjad, q.v.) for the number one. 2˚ (for अ, in Almanacks and Astrology) for Sunday, and Aries. 3˚ (for अ) as a name of the god Vishnu. 4˚ as an inseparable prefix to a word beginning with a consonant, to signify negation or privation: e.g. aśuddha, 'impure,' 'incorrect.' As in Greek, a becomes an before a vowel; e.g. ananta, 'without end,' 'endless.'

adj. Forty. چـﮩل ته chihal-tah, s. f. Coat of mail. چـﮩل ستون chihal-satūn, or چـﮩل منار chihal-manār, n. prop. Name of Persepolis. چـﮩل قدمي chihal-ḳadmī, s. f. 1. Walk, ramble. 2. A custom, at the funerals of Musalmāns, of stepping back forty paces from the grave and again advancing to it before reading the service over.

def: v. n. To be fatigued, to tire.
s. f. Jollity, merriment, cheer, mirth.
adj. H. chār ; Sant. poneyā , S. चतुर Four.

adj. Four. چـﮩ‍ار چند chahārchand, adj. Fourfold. چـﮩ‍ار بالش chahār-bālish, s. m. Throne (Indian) with four cushions. چـﮩ‍ار سو chahār-sū, A square, a marketplace. چـﮩ‍ار شنبه chahār-shamba, Wednesday.
n. m. Wednesday ( budh ). Chahār shamba (budh) na-darād hai . Prov. Wednesday never came to him. (He has no sense)
n. m. Gallop.
s. m. Gallop.
adj. The fourth.
(چه for چاه A well, and بچه A child) s. m. A vat, a cistern.
s. m. A font (for ablution).

n. m. 1. The glad song, warbling, or chirping of birds. Chiṛyāṅ kaise chahehahe kar rahī haiṅ! 2. Mirth; merriment; merry-making; laughter. Āj-kal to yāroṅ ke khūb chahchahe uṛ rahe haiṅ . Now-a-days we chirp all day like a bird.
s. f. The singing or talking (of birds).
v. n. 1. To sing, whistle, warble, chirp (as birds). 2. To chatter or talk in high spirits, cheerily, gaily, etc. 3. To rejoice; exult; make merry.
more: v. n. To blush; glow; shine.

def: s. m. A whistler.
n. m. H. chhāch . The refuse which separates from the butter when melted to make ghī .
s. f. Warbling.
n. f. Land irrigated from wells, etc.
s.f. = ćhāʼīṅ, q.v. s.f. Shade, &c. (ćhāyā); — discoloration; a spectre, ghost.
adj. Beloved; darling.
s. f. A mistress, a sweetheart.

imp. verb. What should be. Must; should; necessary; requisite; required; desirable; due. Ashiq huë haiṅ āp bhī ek aur shakhs par, Ākhir sitam kī kuchh to mukāfāt chāhiye . Gālib. You 've thrown me off, your heart's given to another swain, Requital there must be—my bosom racked with pain. kyā na chāhiye , intj. 1. What dont I want; I want every thing. 2. Nothing is wanting; I have every thing.
n. m. 1. A burn. 2. A layer of stone and cement on a mud floor or roof; pavement. chahkā lagnā , v. n. 1. To be burnt (the skin). 2. To be paved.
more: s. m. 1. A kind of firework. The smarting of a wound, &c. by the applica tion of a medicine. چـﮩکالگانا chahkā lagānā, To burn the skin slightly by fomenting.

def: adj. Brawny, stout.
s. f. Singing or talk of birds.
v. n. See چـﮩکنا chahaknā.

adj. S. छिद्र Wet, oozy (land); miry; puddly; marshy. chahlā , n. m. chahlī , n. f. 1. Wet land; puddle. 2. (Tir. cherā ). A large log of fire wood. 3. A large wooden peg (for tents, etc.) ( mekh ). 4. The hollow in which a pitcher, etc. is fixed. chahle kī bhaiṅs , n. f. lit . A buffalo wallowing in the mire. ( Met .) A fat lazy person.

v. a. 1. To wish; desire. 2. To like; love; to be enamoured of. 1. Mā chāhe beṭī ko, aur beṭī chāhe moṭe ḍhīṅg ko . Prov. The mother on her daughter doats, the daughter on her man robust. 2. Chāhiye achchhoṅ ko jitnā chāhiye; Voh agar chāheṅ to phir kyā chāhiye? Gālib. To win the hearts of good men is a duty meet, If too their love you gain your pleasure is complete. 3. Chāhne ke nām se gadhī ne bhī khet khānā chhoṛ dīyā . Prov. Even the she-ass was scared from her pasture when one talked to her of love. (Follow love and it will flee, flee love and it will follow thee). 3. To approve; choose. 4. To aim or aspire at or after; to pant for. 5. To intend; meditate; propose; offer to do. 6. To will; demand; call for; ask. Allāh chāhe so ho . Every thing happens as God wills.
dakh.) v. a. See چاهنا chāhnā.
Optative, used as a conj. As you will; either; or; whether.

ety: ćhah+oṅ = Prk. ऊहं = S. नाम् (aff. of the gen. plur.)
def: adj. = ćhaʼoṅ, q.v.s.v. ćha. s.f. = ćhāṅw, ćhāṅʼoṅ, ćhāṅh, q.v. s.f. Shade, &c. ( = ćhāṅ, ćhāṅh, q.v.). s.f. Shade; shadow, reflection (of an object in a mirror, &c.)
v. a. (dakh.) To slander, calumniate, asperse.
adj. Due; owing.
s. m. (dakh.) Liking, desire, attachment, will, list.
adj. S. चतुर ( In comp .) Four. chahūṅ or , Poet. adv. On all sides ( chāroṅ taraf ).
adj. Four. چـﮩون اور chahūṅ-or, adv. On all sides. چـﮩون چکريا دس chahūṅ-chakr or -dis ( चतुश्चक्र or चतुर्दिशा ) Circumjacent, all around. ¶ chhoṅ (plur. of چهـ) The six.
s.f. Ashes.
wety: Inherited from Sanskrit क्षार (kṣāra).

def: adj. Removed; abolished; destroyed, &c.
more: 1. (r. क्षि Decay) adj. Abolished. 2. ( छदिः ) s. f. A thatched roof on a boat. 3. A stuffed pad to prevent bullocks being hurt when loaded.

def: s.f. A thatched roof (on a boat, &c.); — a stuffed pad (to prevent oxen, &c. from being galled when loaded)
subs: s.m. A pack-saddle with the pad used with it.

ety: Prk. छइल्लो and छइल्ल ओ; S. छवि+इलः and इल+कः
def: adj. & s.m. Handsome, comely, beautiful; spruce, foppish, gay, dashing; — a handsome fellow a gay young spark, a fop, a beau, an exquisite, a coxcomb
more: s. m. A fop, a beau. چهيل پن chhail-pan, s. m. Spruceness.

ety: Prk. छइल्लो and छइल्ल ओ; S. छवि+इलः and इल+कः
def: adj. & s.m. Handsome, comely, beautiful; spruce, foppish, gay, dashing; — a handsome fellow a gay young spark, a fop, a beau, an exquisite, a coxcomb
more: n. m. S. छविल from छवि beauty. 1. A handsome, dashing youth ( albelā , n. 1, 2.). 2. A gay young fellow; a man of pleasure. 3. A swain; gallant; gay Lothario; Don Juan ( āshnā , 2). Dhobī kā chhailā ādhā ujlā, ādhā mailā . Prov. The washerman's buckish son, half clean, half dirty clothes has on. (He is rigged out from the clean and foul linen of his customers—a coat from one and pants from another). 4. A dandy; fop; coxcomb; an exquisite. Bāhar mīyāṅ chhail chikanyā, ghar meṅ libṛī joë! Prov. Abroad he cuts a dash all o'er the town, at home his wife see in a tattered gown. chhail-pan, chhail-gāī . See bāṅk-pan , and albelāpan , 3 to 6. Chāl bāṅkoṅ kī, pūt faqīrnī kā, dinoṅ shīrnī mol chukāī kare, Ghar khāne ko ās, na pās paisā, jhūṭ mūṭ kī shekhī, baṛāī kare, Karaj-dār phireṅ use bāṅdhne ko, āp galyoṅ meṅ chhail-gāī kare . Kabit. A beggar's son, he moves a swell, for sweets but bargainin…
s. m. A fop, a beau. چهيل پن chhail-pan, s. m. Spruceness.
s. m. (dakh.) A goat, he-goat ( Harris ).
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀙𑀯𑀺 (chavi) + Middle Indo-Aryan -𑀮𑁆𑀮- (-lla-), from Sanskrit छवी (chavī́). Cognate with Odia ଛଇଳା (chaiḷā), Punjabi ਛੈਲਾ /چھَیلا (chailā).

def: Spruceness, foppishness, smartness, jauntiness; gaiety, sportiveness; airs.
Spruceness, foppishness, smartness, jauntiness; gaiety, sportiveness; airs.
Spruceness, foppishness, smartness, jauntiness; gaiety, sportiveness; airs.
s.f. Shade, &c. (ćhāyā); — discoloration; a spectre, ghost.
wety: Inherited from Sanskrit क्षार (kṣāra).

def: s. m. 1. Name of a low class of Hindūs. 2. Seed of tamarind. 3. or چاٴين چوٴين chāʼīṅ chūʼīṅ, s. f. Scaldhead.
n. m. 1. An exacting or litigious person. 2. A low class of Hindus, usually petty thieves. 3. E. Seed of the tamarind. chāëṅ chāëṅ , n. f. 1. Chirping; cawing; chatter. 2. Jabber; gibberish.
n. m. The season of chait , q. v.
adj. Bushy; woody, shady; — s.m. A thicket.
s.m. A kind of flat basket used in winnowing grain; a winnowing fan; — a cow-catcher; splash-board (of a vehicle)
more: n. m. S. छद a leaf. 1. A winnowing fan; a flat dish of basket-work used in winnowing or clearing grain. 1. Chhāj bole to bole, chhalnī bhī bolī jis meṅ bahattar sau chhed! Prov. The winnowing fan may speak if it will, but even the sieve with holes seven thousand and two hundred must give herself a tongue! (Where faults abound you hear the loudest sound). 2. Chhāj ṭūṭ-kar tulyāṅ niksīṅ, kaise nāj pachhoṛūṅ rī? Ab mat te kachh ban nahīṅ ātā, ke apnā sir phoṛūṅ rī . W. Rus. Wom. song. The winnowing fan to pieces worn, how shall I winnow now? In vain I beat my head—the grain I can't sift any how. 2. A cow-catcher. 3. A splash board. Baggī kā chhāj ṭūṭ gayā. chhājāīl' , adj. Having a long beard. chhājoṅ meṅh paṛnā, yā barasnā , v. n. To fall (torrents of rain).
s. m. A kind of basket used in winnowing or clearing grain.

def: adj. Having a long beard.
s.m. Thatching, covering in.
s.m. The expanded branches of a tree (which afford shade); a covered way; eaves (of a house); a pent (over a door-way), a portico; a veranda; a balcony; a gallery
more: n. m. lit . belonging to the chhat roof. 1. The eaves; a sun-shade. 2. A balcony. 3. A peak. chhaj'je-dār , adj. Broad-brimmed (turban).
s. m. 1. Gallery. 2. The expanded branches of a tree.
subs: adj. Having a pent, or a portico, &c.; broad-brimmed (a turban, or hat).

ety: ćhāj˚ = Prk. छाय(इ) = S. छाट्य(ति), rt. छद्
def: v.t. To cover in (a house, &c.), to thatch.
more: v. n. 1. To thatch. 2. To befit, to become.

ety: ćhāj˚ = Prk. छज्ज(इ); S. छदय(ति), rt. छद् or छन्द्
def: v.n. To be becoming (to, -ko), to become, befit, suit.
s.f. Time of milking; milking.
more: Hin. n. f. 1. Milking time. 2. Time. Is chhāk kā dūdh ghanā hai . 3. The first meal in the day. Hālī kī chhāk . 4. A marriage present of large round cakes from the bride to the bridegroom.
s. m. Ready prepared food carried out by labourers and husbandmen when they proceed to their daily work; luncheon.

def: s.m.(f.?) Ready prepared food carried by labourers and husbandmen when they proceed to their daily work; the first meal of the day; luncheon; — large round cakes sent as a marriage present by a bride to the bridegroom.
s. (dakh.) Clashing of swords, battle (?).
adj. Full of liquor, drunk, intoxicated, stupefied; — ćhākṭā, s.m. A drunkard, a sot; an eater of intoxicating drugs (as opium, &c.).
adj. Full, brimful; full to repletion, crammed, satiated; full of wine, intoxicated
subs: (-ko), To fill to repletion, to satisfy, to satiate, to cram.

def: n. f. S. चकितान्ध, H. chāk , cirle, chauṅdhā light. The state of being dazzled. Baṅdhīṅ pagṛiyāṅ tāsh kī sar upar, Chakā-chauṅdh meṅ jis se āve nazar . Mīr Hasan. chakā-chauṅdh lagnā , v. n. To be dazzled.
more: s. f. The state of being dazzled, radiance.

def: s.f. Fulness, repletion, satiety; full satisfaction; — ćhikāʼī, Settlement in full; a deed of release.
v.t. To fill to repletion, to satiate, to pamper, to cloy; to satisfy fully; — to chastise, punish
more: v. a. See chhak'nā . To overtake and seize a run-away. chhakāī , n. f. Fulness; satiety.
(caus. of چهکنا) v. a. 1. To pamper, to cloy, to satiate. 2. To chastise.

def: s. m. Evasion, trick, subterfuge, deceit, shuffle.
v. n. (dakh.) To be in amazement or astonishment.
s. m. A species of cassia (Cassia tora) esteemed a remedy for ringworms.

adj. 1. Well-set; tight; smart. Chāq-chauband sīna-zorī meṅ, Phūl rakkhe huë kaṭorī meṅ . Shauq. * Baṛī pūch honā , 1. To be in great request. 2. To have a long tail or following. Her bosom's swell tight-drawn and smart, A flower upon it placed with art. 2. Athletic; muscular; sinewy. 3. Bold; spirited; dashing. 4. Active; quick; alert; nimble; alive; sharp.
s. f. A musk-rat. (Sorex cærulescens: Shaw. Sorex cæruleus: Turton ).
s. m. The eye.
s. f. Wrangling.
s. f. Discord.

v. a. 1. To enjoy throughly, or with great zest or gusto; to drain the cup of pleasure; to revel; to live while we live. 2. To spend like a prince; to spend every farthing. (The original is very expressive and comprehends, in one word, lavish expenditure for the sake of thorough and immediate enjoyment). Dil kī khushī kī khātir, chākh ḍāl māl dhan ko, Garmard hai to āshiq kauṛī narakh kafan ko . Nazīr. For the heart's delight spend free, in one draught all your wealth enjoy, If man you be and lover true for coffin not a sou lay by. Chhoṭī nandī rī, kuë pānī na jā! nainā lagā koī le jāëgā, Ānkhyāṅ torī nimbuā kī phaṅkyāṅ, nonā lagā koī chakh jāëgā . Wom. song. My tiny sister-in-law, go go not to the well, Eyes meeting eyes transfixed you're borne off by their spell! Your round eyes' zest, lime-halves with salt sucked in compel.
(from چخ) Begone, away, avaunt.
s.m. Aggregate of six; the sixth (at cards, &c.); the six or size (at dice, &c.); — a cage with a net attached to it
more: n. m. S. षटक Six of any thing; the sixth (at cards, etc.). Khūb pau-chhakkā uṛtā hai. chhakkā panjā , Plots; devices; schemes. Chhakkā panjā bhūl gaë. chhakke chhūṭnā , v. n. To lose one's six senses, to be agitated, confounded. Hākim ko dekhte hī chhakke chhūṭ gaë .
s. m. 1. The sixth (at cards, &c.). 2. A cage with a net attached to it. چهکا پنجه کرنا chhakkā panja karnā, To deceive, to play tricks.
subs: s.m. Plots, schemes, devices, tricks; fraud, deceit, cheating
To play tricks, to practise deceit, &c.
v.n. To lose the six senses, to lose all one's wits, to be bewildered, be confounded.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀙𑀓𑁆𑀓 (chakka) + Middle Indo-Aryan -𑀓- (-ka-), from Sanskrit षट्क (ṣáṭka), but influenced by Northwestern Indo-Aryan, for which compare Punjabi چھکا (chakkā) and the other playing card numbers. *chākā would be the expected reflex in Urdu. Cognate with Nepali छक्का (chakkā).

def: v. a. (dakh.) To deceive, to delude.
s.m. (f.?), A thump or blow (especially on the head), a slap, a cuff, a buffet.
more: s. f. A slap, blow, cuff.

def: s. f. Breadth.

n. f. Breadth; width. chaklā-bandī , n. f. The distribution of a Zamīndāri or of a province into chaklās , especially for financial convenience. chakle-dār , n. m. 1. The governor or superintendant of a province or chaklā ; the superintendant, proprietor, or renter of a chaklah . 2. ( Fac .) A pimp.
(چقمق) s. f. A flint.
v.n. To be intoxicated, to be stupefied (cf. ćhāknā).
more: v. a. To clear the water of a well, &c.

ety: prob. for ćhāṅknā; fr. S. स्यन्न (rt. स्यन्द्)+कृ; see ćhānnā
def: v.t. To clear or clean the water of a well; to strain; to purify.
v.n. To be full to repletion, to be satiated, be thoroughly satisfied; to be gratified; — to be astonished; to be harassed, be afflicted; — (dialec.) to be tricked, be deceived
more: v. n. 1. W. To be filled; sated; satiated. 1. Āj to khūb chhake! 2. Sāqī, terī nigāh ne aisā chhakā dīyā; Gam do jahāṅ kā bas tere ek jām se gayā . Muntazir. Cup boy! one glance shot from your eye hath filled my soul, The cark and care of both worlds from me it hath stole. 2. E. To be tricked, deceived.
v. n. See chheknā , v. a.
v. n. 1. To be content or satiated or gratified. 2. To be afflicted, harassed, astonished. چهک جانا chhak jānā, v. n. To be satisfied or satiated.

def: s. (dakh.) Name of a dish, consisting of wheat flour made into paste, formed into small cakes and boiled in water, together with meat, various spices, and salt ( Qanoon-e-islam, gloss.).
adj. (dakh.) Delicious.
(dakh.) Great quickness, with great haste (?).
s.m. A two-wheeled bullock-cart (vulg. called 'a hackery'); a cart (for carrying goods).
more: s. m. A kind of carriage, a cart, car.

def: v.t. To thump, box, slap, cuff, buffet.
more: v. a. To slap, to cuff.

def: s.f. = ćhakkā, q.v. s.m. Aggregate of six; the sixth (at cards, &c.); the six or size (at dice, &c.); — a cage with a net attached to it
subs: s.m. Plots, schemes, devices, tricks; fraud, deceit, cheating
To play tricks, to practise deceit, &c.
v.n. To lose the six senses, to lose all one's wits, to be bewildered, be confounded.

def: n. f. 1. E. A mill ( chakkī ). 2. A pully ( ghirnī ). 3. A bandalore or small wheel with a cord fixed to its centre, which winds and unwinds itself alternately by the motion of the hand. 4. A band of singers.
adj. Full of liquor, drunk, intoxicated, stupefied; — ćhākṭā, s.m. A drunkard, a sot; an eater of intoxicating drugs (as opium, &c.).
n. m. A knife; penknife; a clasp.
more: s. m. A clasp-knife, a penknife.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian چاقو (čāqū).

def: s.m. Division (of crops, &c.) whereby the proprietor (or landlord) receives one-sixth.
more: n. A division of crops in which the zamīṅdār gets one-sixth. chha-konīyā , adj. ( Math .) Hexagon.

def: v.t. To cause to be filled, to cause to be satiated or satisfied.
adj. = ćhail, q.v. (used in comp.)
more: n. f. H. uchhal bound. A wave ( jhāl ); billows; spray.
n. f. S. छल्ली skin. Bark (H. and Sant. baklā ); skin ( khāl ).
see کهال khāl ) s. f. Peel, skin, rind, bark. چهال اتارنا chhāl utārnā, To peel, to decorticate.
subs: adj. & s.m. = ćhail; — s.m. A beautiful species of fragrant moss.
wety: From Sanskrit छल्ली (challī).

ety: S. छल्ली; cf. शल्कं and शल्लं
def: s.f. Skin; rind, peel; bark (cf. khāl)
wety: From Sanskrit छल्ली (challī).

def: s.m. Fraud, deceit, deception, knavery, imposture; sham, pretence, pretext, excuse; circumvention, trick, stratagem, subterfuge, artifice, evasion; guise, disguise, delusion, illusion, semblance, counterfeit, appearance, feint
more: n. f. H. chhalak'nā to overflow. Some bricks, or a piece of a wall fallen out. Dīvār kī chhal gir paṛī. chhal yā sabīl pilānā , v. a. To give a drink of water to passengers. Woh chhal pilātā hai, maiṅ ṭhikāne bhartā hūṅ . He supplies water to travellers, I supply the houses.

n. m. S. छल, Pālī, chhalam . 1. Trick. Dambh sahit kali dharm sab, chhal sammat byohār ; Rāmāyan. Suārth sahit saneh sab, ruchi anuharat achār . 2. A legal quibble; subterfuge; artifice. Tulsī, chhal-bal chhāṛ-ke kīje Rām se neh; Antar pat sūṅ hai kahā jin dekhī sab deh . Tulsī Dās. 3. ( In law .) Imposture; villainy; misdemeanor. chhal batyāṅ , n. f. Specious or deceptive speeches. Rāt saiyāṅ kī chhal batyāṅ maiṅ ne na jānī re . Song. Ṭhumrī. chhal bal , n. m. Trickery.

s. m. Fraud, trick, deception, knavery, artifice, evasion, excuse, subterfuge, pretence. چهل بل chhal bal, s. m. Force and fraud, stratagem, artifice, trick. چهل چهدر يا چهلچهدرم छलछिद्र chhal-chhidr or chhal-chhidram, s. m. Plot, stratagem. چهل چهدري chhal-chhidr-ī, adj. Deceitful, fraudulent.
subs: s.m. Fraud and harm, deceit, &c. ( = ćhal)
s.m. Fraud and force, trick, trickery, stratagem, &c. ( = ćhal)
vulg. ćhal-gyānī, adj. Understanding tricks or strategy, &c.
ćhal-ćhidram, s.m. Trick, plot, stratagem, &c. ( = ćhal)
ćhal-ćhidriyā, adj. Fraudulent, deceitful, tricky, false, &c.
(-ko), To deceive, trick, impose upon, to circumvent, &c. ( = ćhalnā)
v.n. To commit fraud, to practise deceit, &c.
(-ke), To be deceived or tricked (by), be imposed on (by), &c.

def: v. n. 1. To depart this life. 1. Yā zāt meṅ kahāë, nāmī, asīl, zātī, Jamshaidfar ke pote, Naushervāṅ ke nātī, The āp misl-i-dūlah, aur fauj thī barātī, Jab chal base to koī nā suṅg thā na sāthī, Mulk o makāṅ, khazānā, lashkar huā to phir kyā? Nazīr. Be his high birth or caste, or holiness renowned and great, Naushervāṅ just his grandsire or Jamshaid of regal state, As bridegroom glad and gay, his force a wedding party wait, Alone this world hath left, companion none nor intimate, Nor kingdom, palace, treasure, armies then availed him aught. 2. Chal base jo log the islām ke! Rah gaë bāqī Musalmāṅ nām ke! Shahīd. Good Musalmen there were, but they have gone, The name alone's now left this earth upon. 2. To come to an end; to be over. Uskī chāhat n eṅ javānī apnī thī so chal basī . Inshā.
s.f. Spray; a wave, billow ( = jhāl; — in comp. it also takes the form ćhār, e.g. bāʼo-ćhār).
wety: From Sanskrit छल्ली (challī).

def: s.m. Skin, hide (used in comp., e.g. mr̤ig-ćhālā, 'a deer-skin'); — a blister; a pustule, pimple
more: n. m. 1. (In comp.) Skin; hide. Mirg-chhālā . 2. A blister ( phapholā ). Mere khodat ghās paṛā chhālā . Rus.
see کهالا khālā ) s. m. A blister, a pustule, pimple, skin.
subs: A blister to rise, to become blistered.

def: s.f. Running over, overflow; splashing, splash.
more: n. f. S. उच्चलत Running over; overflow.

def: adj. & s.m. Deceptive, delusive; deceiving, cheating; — a deceiver, knave, cheat
subs: adj. & s.m. Deceptive, false, feigning, artful, tricky; — a deceitful or tricky person, &c.

ety: ćhalak˚ = Prk. छलक्क(इ) or छलक्के(इ), fr. S. उद्+शल्+कृ
def: v.n. To be spilt; to overflow; to spill; to splash; — v.t. To fill to the brim
more: v. n. To splash; to be spilt; to spill. Haule bharūṅ merā bālak rove, jaldī chalūṅ merī chhalke gāgariyā . Wom. song. If at the well I slowly draw my child acrying keeps, if I walk fast the water in my pitcher makes a splash. chhalak'nā , v. a. To fill to the brim. Zarā kalsā chhalak denā .
v. a. 1. See chhulchhulānā . (3.). 2. To discharge semen.
(see چهلک chhalak ) v. n. To be spilt, to spill, to overflow.
v. a. To make water slowly (as, mares, &c. in heat).
subs: v.t. To fill to the brim.

def: part. adj. (f. -ī), Running over
s.m. Deceiving, deluding, tricking, cheating, outwitting; feigning (see ćhalnā).

ety: contrac. fr. S. उद्+शल्+लङ्घं; cf. ućhalnā
def: s.f. Skipping, jumping; skip, leap, spring, bound
more: n. f. See پهلانگ chhalāṅg'nā, chhalāṅg mārnā , v. n. See phalāṅgnā .
s. f. Skipping, jumping, a spring, skip, leap. چهلانگين مارنا chhalāngeṅ mārnā, To spring, to jump.
subs: (or ćhalāṅgeṅ) mārnā or ćhalāng bharnā, v.n. To skip, jump, spring, &c. ( = ćhalāṅgnā).

def: s.f. = ćhalāṅg, q.v. s.f. Skipping, jumping; skip, leap, spring, bound
subs: (or ćhalāṅgeṅ) mārnā or ćhalāng bharnā, v.n. To skip, jump, spring, &c. ( = ćhalāṅgnā).

def: v.n. = ćhalāṅgnā, q.v. v.n. To skip, jump, leap, spring, bound.
v.n. To skip, jump, leap, spring, bound.

adj. 1. About to depart, run away, or abscond. 2. Dying ( marne ko ); expiring; in extremis . Phr. At death's door; on one's death-bed; at the point of death; near one's end; death staring one in the face; having one foot in the grave; one's days being numbered. Woh ab chalan-hāroṅ meṅ haiṅ .
(from چلنا q. v.) adj. 1. Going. 2. Frail, inconstant, fickle. 3. On the point of death.
adj. (dakh.) in compos. Causing to move or go; impeller.
s.m. Ignis fatuus, will-o-th'-wisp
more: n. m. H. chhalnā , to deceive. Ignis fatuus ; will-o'th' wisp. Chhalāvā sā idhar udhar phirtā hai. chhalāvā ho jānā , v. n. 1. To disappear; vanish suddenly; decamp. 2. To elude search or pursuit.
subs: v.n. 'To turn into or be like a will-o-thʼ-wisp,' to vanish or disappear suddenly, to elude search or pursuit.

def: adj. 1. Disordered; disarranged; out of place. 2. Disjointed; out of joint; unhinged; ricketty. Gar ho Khudā na-khuāstah! ek kal bhī chal-bichal , Nazīr. Phir na khushī, na aish, na kuchh zindgī kā phal .
more: n. f. Displacement; disorganization; topsy-turvy; disarrangement; disorder; panic. Majlis meṅ chal-bichal ye paṛī tab khabar paṛī. chalpukār' , n. f. Hue and cry; shout.

def: s.f. Rustling or rippling sound; babble, murmur.

ety: fr. S. शल् or क्षल् = क्षर्, redupl.
def: v.n. To move with a rustling sound ( = sarsarānā); to make a rippling sound; to babble, to murmur; — to come off (as loose fæces) with a rumbling interrupted noise; — ćhulćhulānā, To make water slowly (as a mare, &c. in heat; cf. ćhulaknā)
more: v. a. To make water slowly (as, mares, &c. in heat).
subs: v.n. To move about making a rustling or rumbling noise; to flit about restlessly.

ety: ćhal(nā)+hāʼī = Prk. एअव्वअइओ (with h inserted) = S. (इ)तव्य+क+इकः (इन्+कः)
def: adj. Deceitful, tricky, &c. ( = ćhaliyā, q.v.). s.f. Betel-nut.
s.f. A small blister, &c.
adj. & s.m. Fraudulent, deceitful, false, perfidious, treacherous; artful, tricky; — a deceiver, cheat, trickster, betrayer, &c.
s.m. A song consisting of four parts and recited with certain gestures and gesticulation.
s.f. Betel-nut.
more: n. f. S. छटाफल Betelnut ( phāl ).
s. f. A kind of betel-nut.

def: adj. & s.m. Fraudulent, deceitful, false, perfidious, treacherous; artful, tricky; — a deceiver, cheat, trickster, betrayer, &c.
more: adj. Tricky; deceitful; fraudulent; false; treacherous; perfidious.
adj. Deceitful, fraudulent, artful, treacherous, false, perfidious, betrayer.

def: (caus. of ćhalaknā), v.t To cause to run over, to spill.
more: (trans. of چهلکنا chhalaknā ) v. a. To spill.

def: s.m. A plain ring (of gold, silver, or other metal, worn on a finger or toe)
more: n. m. 1. A ring. 2. A ring worn on the finger or toe. Chhallā de more tāīṅ . Hījṛās' song. Chhallā . 3. A kind of song sung by eunuchs at the birth of a child. 4. The title of the most popular Panjābī air. One of the verses sung to this air runs as follows. Chhallā raṅg baraṅgī, āyā Lāṭ Firangī, Udī men hai chaṅgī, balā, balā rāyā, chhallā ḍāḍā lāyā . Panj. chhalle-dār , adj. 1. Ringed ( annulata ); annular. 2. Articulate; vertebrate. 3. Greasy (broth). Chhalle-dār shurvā. tel kā chhallā , A drop of oil floating on a liquid. Niṅbū kā araq tel ke chhalle se ṭhahartā hai .
s. m. A ring (ornamental). چهلي دار chhalle-dār, adj. Annular.
s. m. A thin wiry metallick ring, commonly worn on the thumb or great toe.
subs: s.m. A game in which one of a party or side hides a ring in his hand, and one of an opposite party has to guess in whose hand the ring is
s.m. A ring consisting of three rings joined together, the two outer ones being plain, and the middle one having interstices
adj. Having rings; ringed, annular; — articulate; vertebrate; — having rings or spots of grease or oil on the surface (as soup, water, &c.), greasy.

def: s.m. A kind of song (usually sung by eunuchs on the occasion of the birth of a child); — name of an air or song (popular in the Panjāb).

ety: prob. a Prākr̤it form; cf. शल्कं and शल्लं
def: s.f. Skin; bark; rind, &c. ( = ćhilkā; cf. jhillī).
more: adj. Deceitful, fraudulent, artful, treacherous, false, perfidious, betrayer.

ety: ćhal˚ = Prk. छल(इ) or छले(इ) = S. छलय(ति), fr. the n. छल
def: v.t. To deceive, delude, trick, cheat, outwit, circumvent, impose on; to evade; to feign, pretend; to represent jalsely; to personate.
more: v. a. S. छल्लि+लू from छल्लि skin लू to cut. 1. To be peeled, husked, pared, shelled, excoriated, scraped. 2. To be erased, struck out, obliterated.
v. a. S. छल 1. To impose on; circumvent ( buttā denā , 1.). 2. To represent falsely; to personate.
v. a. To deceive, to cheat, to evade, to make excuses.
v. a. (dakh.) To excoriate, to flay, erase, obliterate, scrape, pare ( Harris ): see چهيلنا chhīlnā.

def: s.m. A strainer, a cloth through which anything is sifted (see ćhalnī).
s.f. An instrument for skinning or peeling.
s.f. A sieve, a strainer ( = ćhālnī, ćalnī); — adj. Battered
more: n. f. S. चालनी winnowing. A sieve; a strainer. chhalnī h. , v. n. 1. To have holes or to be pierced through like a sieve. 2. To be riddled, battered. Jinke naë naë the makāṅ aur mahal-sarā, Unkī chhateṅ ṭapaktī haiṅ chhalnī ho jā ba-jā . Nazīr.
1. adj. Battered. 2. s. f. (Sans. चालनी ) A sieve. چهلني کرنا chhalnī karnā, To batter.
subs: v.n. To be pierced or perforated, to be full of holes (like a sieve); to be riddled, be battered.

def: s. m. Motion.
